title: Deaths in US immigration custody must be investigated: UN rights chief
content:
UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk on Friday called for stronger independent oversight of the United States immigration detention system and investigations into all deaths in US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) custody. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Deaths in US immigration custody must be investigated: UN rights chief appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Global drug use reaches record high as increasingly potent synthetic drugs spread
content:
More people are using drugs than ever before, while synthetic substances are reshaping illicit markets and exposing vulnerable communities to greater health risks, according to the UN World Drug Report 2026, released on Friday. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Global drug use reaches record high as increasingly potent synthetic drugs spread appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Lives at risk in DR Congo as Ebola outbreak continues to outpace response
content:
The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) continues to spread faster than aid efforts can keep pace, despite significant gains in treatment capacity and growing community engagement, the World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Wednesday. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Lives at risk in DR Congo as Ebola outbreak continues to outpace response appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Ebola in DR Congo: first month of outbreak sees record number of cases – UN humanitarians
content:
Ebola has been spreading at unprecedented speed in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), bringing risk and fear into people’s daily lives, UN humanitarians said on Tuesday.  Read Full Story on UN News

content:

title: Global Ebola cases top 1,000 as UN races to reach DR Congo’s most vulnerable
content:
As global confirmed Ebola cases reach 1,000, nearly three million children and adolescents are at risk in the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while efforts increase to treat prisoners near the epicentre of the current outbreak, UN agencies warned on Monday. Read Full Story on UN News

title: Ebola in DR Congo: One month on, scaled up response remains insufficient
content:

title: DR Congo: Efforts ramp up as Ebola outbreak accelerates beyond borders
content:
The spread of Ebola is accelerating in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) amid continued armed violence, posing a grave and growing risk to the region, UN agencies warned on Friday. Read Full Story on UN News

The post DR Congo: Efforts ramp up as Ebola outbreak accelerates beyond borders appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.
